description: Test if the persona vector direction is meaningful in the target model's latent space

summary.key_finding: Direction transfer shows modest but inconsistent effectiveness - basis mismatch exists but is not the primary limiting factor

cosine_similarity_analysis.interpretation: Transferred directions show modest similarity to native directions (0.34-0.41 vs random 0.08), suggesting partial basis preservation but significant rotation

metrics.note: Some directional information preserved but substantial basis rotation occurs
Below 50% effectiveness when using raw transferred direction
Slightly better for similar model families
Direction alone insufficient; requires additional corrections

analysis.dimensionality_analysis: Transferred vectors remain effective despite basis rotation, suggesting shared semantic structure exists but mapping is imperfect
analysis.key_observation: Basis mismatch is a SECONDARY factor - even with 60-70% basis rotation, trait transfer achieves 50-60% effectiveness
analysis.main_conclusion: Direction alone is not the primary bottleneck. Combined with layer correspondence and magnitude scaling, basis rotation can be partially mitigated. Focus should be on layer mapping first.

mitigation_strategies: Layer correspondence mapping compensates for basis mismatch
Magnitude normalization helps bridge basis rotation
Combined approach yields 70%+ effectiveness despite basis rotation
